tests.source_program:
define i1 @icmp_equality_test_pointer_types(i8* %X, i8* %Y, i8* %Z) {
entry:
  %XeqZ = icmp eq i8* %X, %Z
  %YeqZ = icmp eq i8* %Y, %Z
  %XeqY = icmp eq i8* %X, %Y
  %not.YeqZ = xor i1 %YeqZ, true
  %and = select i1 %not.YeqZ, i1 %XeqY, i1 false
  %equal = select i1 %XeqZ, i1 %YeqZ, i1 %and
  ret i1 %equal
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions i64 and i42 integer types but does not explicitly cover pointer type comparisons. While icmp works on pointers, this specific type variation is not documented in the scope.

define i1 @icmp_equality_test_pointer_types(i8* %X, i8* %Y, i8* %Z) {
entry:
  %XeqZ = icmp eq i8* %X, %Z
  %YeqZ = icmp eq i8* %Y, %Z
  %XeqY = icmp eq i8* %X, %Y
  %not.YeqZ = xor i1 %YeqZ, true
  %and = select i1 %not.YeqZ, i1 %XeqY, i1 false
  %equal = select i1 %XeqZ, i1 %YeqZ, i1 %and
  ret i1 %equal
}

  %YeqZ = icmp eq i128 %Y, %Z
  %XeqY = icmp eq i128 %X, %Y
  %not.YeqZ = xor i1 %YeqZ, true
  %and = select i1 %not.YeqZ, i1 %XeqY, i1 false
  %equal = select i1 %XeqZ, i1 %YeqZ, i1 %and
  ret i1 %equal
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows i64 and i42 examples but does not explicitly mention very wide integer types like i128. This tests the pattern with a significantly different bit width.

tests.source_program:
define i1 @icmp_equality_test_and_instruction(i64 %X, i64 %Y, i64 %Z) {
entry:
  %XeqZ = icmp eq i64 %X, %Z
  %YeqZ = icmp eq i64 %Y, %Z
  %XeqY = icmp eq i64 %X, %Y
  %not.YeqZ = xor i1 %YeqZ, true
  %and = and i1 %not.YeqZ, %XeqY
  %equal = select i1 %XeqZ, i1 %YeqZ, i1 %and
  ret i1 %equal
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions select-with-swapped-operands for the inner operation but does not explicitly cover using a direct 'and' instruction instead of select for the logical AND operation.
